title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. maura
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. maura, also known as madder, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to its high content of anthraquinones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of madder include reducing inflammation and pain, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular herbal remedy for various conditions. Madder can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used as a natural dye due to its vibrant red color.
